caveats: The user may have run verification outside indexed agent sessions.

evidence_refs.label: No fresh command/test/browser proof found in compact history

open_questions: What is the smallest relevant command or browser task for this diff?
suggested_checks: Attach a fresh command, test, log, screenshot, or trace before marking the finding fixed.
evidence_candidates.why_it_matters: A review without fresh verification evidence can only prove static plausibility, not that the change works.

evidence_procedure_steps.action: Choose the smallest relevant repo command or browser task, rerun it, and attach the fresh output.
evidence_procedure_steps.artifact: command log, test report, screenshot, trace, or QA run artifact
evidence_procedure_steps.gate: No candidate is marked confirmed or fixed using stale or missing verification.
evidence_procedure_steps.input: Failed, stale, or missing command/test/browser evidence.
evidence_procedure_steps.output: Fresh pass/fail evidence linked to the review.

findings.suggestion: Return a Result/Option and use checked_sub: `self.balance = self.balance.checked_sub(amount).ok_or(Error::InsufficientFunds)?;` (or saturating_sub only if clamp-to-zero is the intended policy). Never use bare `-=` on a balance.
findings.summary: source.rs:17 `self.balance -= amount;` performs unchecked u64 subtraction. When amount > balance, debug builds panic and release builds wrap to a near-u64::MAX value, so an over-debit produces a huge balance instead of failing — a balance-integrity/money-creation defect and a debug-build panic DoS. No callers exist yet, but the broken contract is inherited by any future caller.
findings.title: debit() underflow creates phantom balance on over-debit

findings.suggestion: Choose explicit semantics: `self.balance = self.balance.checked_add(amount).ok_or(Overflow)?;` to reject, or `saturating_add` to cap. Add a test at u64::MAX.
findings.summary: Line 11 `self.balance += amount;` performs unchecked u64 addition. In debug it panics on overflow; in release it wraps, so a large credit can silently roll the balance down to a small value (BUG comment lines 8-10).
findings.title: credit: unchecked addition — overflow panics (debug) or wraps (release)

findings.suggestion: Guard funds and use checked_sub: `let new = self.balance.checked_sub(amount).ok_or(InsufficientFunds)?;` returning a Result/Option instead of mutating on underflow. Add a test for amount > balance.
findings.summary: Line 17 `self.balance -= amount;` has no `amount <= self.balance` guard. In debug an over-debit panics (DoS); in release it underflows and wraps to a near-u64::MAX balance, so debiting more than the balance effectively creates money. Confirmed by the BUG comment at lines 15-16 and the missing bounds check.
findings.title: debit: unchecked subtraction — over-debit panics (debug) or wraps to huge balance (release)

findings.suggestion: Return a `Result`/`Option` and use `self.balance.checked_sub(amount)`, rejecting the over-debit (e.g. `self.balance = self.balance.checked_sub(amount).ok_or(Error::InsufficientFunds)?;`). Do not rely on debug overflow-checks, which are off in release.
findings.summary: source.rs:17 `self.balance -= amount;` has no check that `amount <= self.balance`. When a caller debits more than the balance, release builds wrap the u64 to a near-u64::MAX value (silent money creation) and debug builds panic (DoS). The implicit invariant that a debit cannot exceed the balance is stated in intent (comment lines 15-16) but never enforced. `debit` is a `pub` method intended for reuse despite 0 current callers.
findings.title: debit() underflow: no balance>=amount guard, wraps to huge balance in release

findings.suggestion: Use checked_add and surface the overflow: `self.balance = self.balance.checked_add(amount).ok_or(Error::Overflow)?;` (or saturating_add if capping is intended). Add a unit test asserting overflow is rejected rather than wrapped.
findings.summary: source.rs:11 `self.balance += amount;` performs unchecked u64 addition. When balance + amount exceeds u64::MAX, debug builds panic and release builds wrap, dropping the balance to a small number — silent fund loss. The in-code comment (lines 8-10) confirms the intent to fix this.
findings.title: credit() overflow silently rolls balance back to a small value

findings.suggestion: Use `self.balance = self.balance.checked_add(amount).ok_or(Error::Overflow)?;` (or `saturating_add` if capping is acceptable) so behavior is identical and safe in debug and release.
findings.summary: source.rs:11 `self.balance += amount;` performs unchecked addition. In release, overflowing u64 wraps a large balance back to a small value; in debug it panics. The comment (lines 8-10) explicitly says to use checked_add/saturating_add but the code still uses raw `+=`, so the stated remediation was not applied.
findings.title: credit() unchecked addition can panic (debug) or wrap (release)

findings.suggestion: Do not treat the BUG comments as sign-off. Fix the arithmetic, remove the BUG comments, add tests for overflow/underflow paths, and run `cargo test` to attach fresh verification before marking resolved.
findings.summary: Both methods carry `// BUG:` comments (lines 8-10, 15-16) that precisely describe the defect, yet the buggy `+=`/`-=` code was committed unchanged. No unit tests exist and no verification artifact was attached (unresolved `rerun_relevant_verification` gate / `no-fresh-verification-evidence`). The next agent could mistake these self-documented bugs for accepted behavior.
findings.title: Incomplete handoff: bugs documented in comments but left unfixed, with no tests or verification

nodes.label: Prior commits, decisions, agents, and command evidence

nodes.detail: blocked · No candidate is marked confirmed or fixed using stale or missing verification.
nodes.label: rerun_relevant_verification

summary:
Risk-tiered review (lite) completed.

New financial primitive `Account` with `credit`/`debit` using unchecked u64 arithmetic. Both bugs are real and match their own comments — but the comments' recommended fix (checked_add/saturating_add) was never applied, so intent contradicts implementation. debit() is the more serious: it lacks any `amount <= balance` guard, so a normal over-debit wraps to a huge balance in release (money creation) or panics in debug. Impact is currently latent (0 callers) but these are pub methods clearly meant for reuse. Not verified by running cargo; Rust overflow semantics are well-established, but a unit test asserting over-debit is rejected remains open work.

New 20-line financial primitive with two real integer-overflow defects that the code's own comments flag. debit() (line 17) uses unchecked `-=`: an over-debit underflows to a near-u64::MAX balance in release (money creation) and panics in debug. credit() (line 11) uses unchecked `+=`: overflow wraps the balance down to a small value (silent fund loss) in release and panics in debug. Both are provable from Rust arithmetic semantics. Blast radius is currently 0 callers (newly added file), so no live user flow is broken today, but the primitive's contract is fundamentally unsafe and any future caller inherits it. Verification is static-only: the repo has no Cargo.toml, build target, or tests, so the `rerun_relevant_verification` gate cannot be satisfied in-tree.

Newly added 20-line file with two genuine integer-overflow bugs on financial balances: unchecked `+=` in credit (line 11) and unchecked `-=` in debit (line 17, no insufficient-funds guard). Both panic in debug and wrap in release — debit underflow can inflate a balance to near-u64::MAX. The agent documented both bugs in comments but shipped them unfixed, with no tests and no verification evidence. Blast radius is currently 0 callers (unused), which limits immediate impact, but the API is broken by construction.
